opp.next_step: JRA 6/29: Email from Michelle, Director of Procurement. Zycus has been selected as a top two finalist for their S2P transformation project. Next steps could include an additional demo, pricing breakdowns, and implementation discussions. The delay is because they had to make a "netsuite decision" which we offered to help with. Their timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace. Additionally, there is new IT leadership. Jason, project lead is currently facilitating a knowledge transfer of the project before we move forward with next steps.
opp.ais_why:
• This is an active RFP with AI sitting at the center. The 4/20/2026 demo featured AI-powered Merlin Intake for regulated service requests, AI-generated sourcing templates and question libraries (the rep cited roughly 40% faster event build), Effects Scoring Pro using AI to suggest supplier evaluation scores, and Agentic CLM auto-extracting contract metadata. Mark Hannigan was impressed that Merlin Intake configured a complex use case in just two hours.
• More than 20 stakeholders, including Michelle Messier, Daniel Pires, Jason Howard, Chad Berryhill, and Kimberly Roberts, engaged on AI specifics. They probed the supplier-scoring AI logic and got the explanation that it understands the event scope and evaluates every supplier response. Mark also reacted with "Impressive" to multi-ERP integration.
• The RFP is rated at the top tier, Autonomous and Agentic AI (Outcomes), which matches the demo content. With an estimated $500k CRO clinical-trial value, a regulated environment driving the need for AI orchestration, audit-grade two-envelope bidding, and Supplier Risk Radar in play, the full agentic AI value prop landed.

champion.narrative: Regina Oliver (Head of Procurement, Operations) is the primary champion candidate. She led the 2026-04-20 demo call, articulated the CRO spend pain ($500k), asked detailed questions about Zycus capabilities, and is the confirmed evaluator (Contact Role: Evaluator). However, her champion strength is developing, not strong. There is no evidence of active internal advocacy: Regina has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer or CIO, has not shared internal evaluation scorecards or competitive intel, has not coached Zycus on how to position against the other finalist, and has not pushed back on competitor positioning in Zycus's favor. Her engagement is active (she participated in the demo) but her advocacy is unproven. Michelle (Director of Procurement) is a second potential champion: she sent the top-two finalist email (2026-06-29, source: Next_Step__c), signaling she owns the vendor selection decision at the procurement leadership level. However, Michelle has not appeared on any Avoma call, so her engagement level, access to the EB, and willingness to advocate for Zycus are unknown. She may be executing the RFP process neutrally rather than championing Zycus. The champion gap is a forecast risk: without a strong internal advocate who can navigate the EB approval, coach Zycus on the competitive landscape, and drive urgency through the IT leadership transition, the deal is vulnerable to passive evaluation, competitor coaching, or decision paralysis. The recommended move is to deepen Regina's advocacy by co-building the quantified ROI case with her (making her the co-author and internal sponsor of the business case) and to request an introduction to Michelle for a direct conversation on competitive positioning and EB access.

competition.narrative: Zycus is competing in a top-two finalist scenario against one unnamed vendor, with Coupa (incumbent), GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, and SAP Ariba as the broader competitive set (source: Salesforce Competitor field). Coupa is the primary competitive force: the buyer is replacing Coupa, and the urgency to close is driven by the Coupa replacement timeline ('Their timeline remains tight because of the Coupa replace', Next_Step__c 2026-06-29). The buyer's dissatisfaction with Coupa (implied by the replace decision) is a wedge for Zycus, but the specific reasons for the Coupa exit have not been surfaced, so Zycus cannot yet tailor the anti-Coupa positioning. The identity of the other top-two finalist is unknown, which is a blind spot: Zycus does not know whether the head-to-head is against GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba, or another vendor, and therefore cannot prepare finalist-specific differentiation or anticipate the competitor's likely moves. The NetSuite integration capability is a confirmed competitive wedge: Zycus demonstrated prebuilt iSaaS middleware with 1,100+ APIs during the 2026-04-20 demo, and the buyer's decision to shortlist Zycus to the top two signals that integration confidence is a differentiator. The recommended move is to surface the competitor identity and the Coupa exit reasons explicitly in the next buyer conversation (with Regina or Michelle): ask 'What drove the decision to move off Coupa, and how are you evaluating the other finalist against those same criteria?' This intel will unlock tailored positioning for the pricing and final demo discussions.

reasons.detail: Roivant Sciences ($225k S2P, forecast 'Upside Key Deal', stage Shortlisted) had its close date pushed from Jun 25 to Jul 29 on 2026-06-22 by owner Justin Ajmo. The buyer is stalled on an internal NetSuite architecture decision and IT knowledge transfer that must finish before pricing, an additional demo, and implementation scoping can proceed, so the new date rides entirely on their internal timeline. The deal is genuinely live (Zycus named top-two finalist on 6/29, replacing incumbent Coupa on a tight timeline), but with no Economic Buyer yet identified the forecast date can slip again and this quarter-key deal could slide out of the quarter.

north_star_verdict.headline: Top-two finalist for $225k S2P transformation with 29 days to close, but buyer paused on internal NetSuite decision and IT leadership knowledge transfer before resuming pricing, additional demo, and implementation discussions. No Economic Buyer mapped, no pricing delivered, no commercial discussions started. Path to close requires pricing finalization, possible additional demo, EB approval, implementation scoping, NetSuite integration validation, legal/contracting — realistically 8 to 10 weeks of work compressed into 29 days.

flags: Map the Economic Buyer immediately. No EB identified across contact roles or calls. A Director of Procurement (Michelle) sent the finalist email, but final budget authority for $225k SaaS at a clinical-stage biopharma typically sits with CFO or VP Finance/Operations. Ask Regina or Michelle directly who approves the budget and commercial terms, and request an introductory call with that person before pricing is delivered. The EB gap is the single highest forecast risk.

Build a quantified ROI model with the buyer to arm the champion and EB. Buyer articulated clear operational pain (manual processes, approval delays, limited sourcing access) and estimated $500k CRO services spend, but no hard savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been quantified. Co-create a savings model with Regina/Jason that ties Merlin AI automation (intake, sourcing templates, scoring, risk monitoring) to measurable cost and time reductions, so the EB has a defensible business case for approval.

Accelerate NetSuite integration confidence to unlock the buyer's paused decision. The buyer is stalled on an internal NetSuite architecture decision (source: Next_Step__c 2026-06-23), and NetSuite integration is a confirmed go/no-go criterion. Offer a technical proof-of-concept, a reference customer on NetSuite (ideally in biopharma or regulated services), or a joint workshop with the buyer's NetSuite admin and IT leadership to prove the iSaaS middleware integration is turnkey. This is the unlock to restart pricing and implementation discussions.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance/Operations) immediately. Michelle (Director of Procurement) sent the finalist email, but Director level does not hold final budget authority for $225k. EB approval is the unlock to commercial sign-off and contracting. Without EB access, the deal will stall post-pricing or lose to a competitor with stronger C-level sponsorship.

Offer a technical NetSuite integration validation session with the buyer's IT and NetSuite teams to unblock their internal architecture decision. Zycus demonstrated prebuilt iSaaS middleware (2026-04-20), but the buyer has not validated it internally and paused next steps pending this decision (Next_Step__c 2026-06-29). Integration confidence is a confirmed go/no-go criterion. A live proof-of-concept or technical deep-dive with IT will restart momentum and de-risk the buyer's internal approval.

Co-build a quantified ROI business case with procurement and finance to arm the Economic Buyer with a defensible financial justification. The buyer articulated operational pain and estimated $500k CRO spend (2026-04-20), but no hard savings, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been quantified. EB approval at $225k ACV requires a business case. Run a ROI workshop with Regina (procurement) and Jess Marenco or Jessica Craft (AP operations) to calculate savings from automated intake, sourcing, and invoice matching, then present to the EB as the centerpiece of the commercial proposal.

Map and engage the Economic Buyer (likely CFO or VP Finance/Operations) immediately. Michelle (Director of Procurement) sent the finalist email, but Director-level does not hold final $225k budget authority at a clinical-stage biopharma. EB approval is the gate to contracting and the single highest forecast risk. Recommend: ask Regina Oliver or Michelle to introduce us directly to the CFO or VP Finance for a commercial and ROI validation conversation within the next 7 days.

Run a competitive review with Regina Oliver or Michelle to confirm Coupa's weaknesses and tighten our AI/integration positioning. Coupa is the incumbent and head-to-head finalist — buyer knows them well. No recent competitive intel on Coupa's pricing, roadmap, or internal sentiment post-finalist notification (29 May, 32 days ago). Zycus must demonstrate differentiated AI value (Merlin Intake 2-hour config vs weeks of custom build, Effects Scoring Pro, Supplier Risk Radar) and NetSuite integration ease (prebuilt iSaaS middleware, 1,100+ APIs) to displace the incumbent. Recommend: schedule a 30-minute competitive debrief with Regina or Michelle within the next 14 days to surface Coupa's gaps and confirm our wedge.

Deliver pricing with a co-built ROI case to unlock EB approval. Buyer explicitly stated next steps include 'pricing breakdowns' (Next_Step__c 29 Jun), but no pricing has been presented. EB will need a defensible business case (hard savings, cycle-time reduction, compliance cost avoidance) to approve $225k spend. Recommend: draft a pricing proposal anchored to the $500k CRO services spend and operational pain articulated on 20 Apr demo, co-build quantified ROI with Regina or Michelle, and deliver within the next 14 days (once buyer completes NetSuite decision and IT knowledge transfer).

Map and engage Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Finance/Operations) within 7 days. Finalist status and pricing are meaningless without EB sign-off, and EB is unmapped 29 days from close. Secure a direct EB call to present the business case (operational pain, $500k CRO spend, AI ROI) and secure budget approval. EB gap is the single highest forecast risk.

Convert Regina Oliver or Michelle (Director of Procurement) from passive evaluator to active champion. Develop competitive coaching (how to position Zycus AI vs. GEP/peer finalist on ROI and NetSuite fit) and secure their help introducing the EB and driving urgency. Without an active champion, deal risks neutral RFP execution and selection on price/vendor comfort instead of AI value.

Co-build a quantified ROI model with finance stakeholders (Jess Marenco, EB once mapped) anchored to the $500k CRO spend and operational pain (cycle-time reduction, headcount savings, compliance cost avoidance). Buyer articulated qualitative pain but no hard savings target. EB will need a defensible business case to approve $225k investment. ROI co-creation is the unlock to EB approval and competitive differentiation.

hard.ais_why:
• This is an active RFP with AI sitting at the center. The 4/20/2026 demo featured AI-powered Merlin Intake for regulated service requests, AI-generated sourcing templates and question libraries (the rep cited roughly 40% faster event build), Effects Scoring Pro using AI to suggest supplier evaluation scores, and Agentic CLM auto-extracting contract metadata. Mark Hannigan was impressed that Merlin Intake configured a complex use case in just two hours.
• More than 20 stakeholders, including Michelle Messier, Daniel Pires, Jason Howard, Chad Berryhill, and Kimberly Roberts, engaged on AI specifics. They probed the supplier-scoring AI logic and got the explanation that it understands the event scope and evaluates every supplier response. Mark also reacted with "Impressive" to multi-ERP integration.
• The RFP is rated at the top tier, Autonomous and Agentic AI (Outcomes), which matches the demo content. With an estimated $500k CRO clinical-trial value, a regulated environment driving the need for AI orchestration, audit-grade two-envelope bidding, and Supplier Risk Radar in play, the full agentic AI value prop landed.
